name: iRobot Roomba j7+
summary: A self-emptying robot vacuum with iRobot's PrecisionVision AI obstacle avoidance — trained to recognize and navigate around cords, socks, shoes, and pet waste — paired with a Clean Base that holds 60 days of debris.
features: PrecisionVision AI obstacle avoidance (cords, socks, pet waste)
Clean Base Automatic Dirt Disposal (self-empties for up to 60 days)
Keep Out Zones and Clean Zones (app-defined)
Alexa and Google Assistant voice control
Two rubber brush rolls (tangle-free for pet hair)
iRobot OS software with ongoing feature updates
Pet Owner Official Promise (re-cleans or refunds if it fails to avoid pet waste)
pros: PrecisionVision AI reliably avoids cables, pet waste, and small objects competitors miss
Self-emptying Clean Base means up to 60 days of hands-off operation
Smart Mapping accurately divides home into named rooms for targeted cleaning
Dual rubber brush rolls dramatically reduce hair tangles vs. bristle brushes
iRobot OS receives regular OTA updates with new obstacle recognition training
cons: Clean Base requires bag replacements (ongoing cost ~$10–$15 per bag)
75-minute battery is shorter than some competitors for large homes
Initial mapping run takes several sessions to fully learn the home
Premium price point compared to capable budget robot vacuums
bestFor: Pet owners concerned about waste avoidance
Households with charging cables and cluttered floors
People who want truly hands-off vacuuming for 1–2 months at a time
Smart home users with Alexa or Google Home setups
notBestFor: Very large homes over 2,000 sq ft (battery may not cover in one run)
Budget-conscious buyers — capable alternatives exist for under $300
Users who prefer self-contained units without a base station
